channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Word of the Day: insuperable
item.description: This word has appeared in 12 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: No envíes cheques por correo. Simplemente no lo hagas.
item.description: Los expertos recomiendan revisar con frecuencia los movimientos de tu cuenta. Los cheques robados de los buzones pueden ser alterados y utilizados para cometer fraude.
item.dc:creator: Ann Carrns

item.title: With Lessons From a Perilous Blizzard, Mamdani Prepares for Extreme Heat
item.description: After more than two dozen people died during a brutal cold snap in New York City, city officials came up with a plan to keep people safe in the cold and extreme heat.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman and Caitlyn Freeman

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani held a news conference on extreme heat preparedness at New York City Emergency Management Headquarters.

item.title: Back in Congress, Tom Kean Jr. Faces a New Challenge: Staying There
item.description: The New Jersey Republican has explained the reason for his long absence: depression. Now he must convince voters that he deserves another term.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr. of New Jersey.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 1, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Turkey as a Tango Destination? A Reporter Was Living That Story.
item.description: A Times journalist profiled Istanbul’s thriving tango scene after finding herself transformed by the dance.
item.dc:creator: Safak Timur

media:content.media:credit: Laura Boushnak for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Safak Timur, a reporter in The Times’s Istanbul bureau, dancing at a tango school in Istanbul. Aficionados say they can identify Turks by their dancing.

item.title: Her Letter Is a Window Into the Dawn of the American Revolution
item.description: A young New Yorker’s letter to her cousin in London showed how colonial women in the late 1700s were waging their own battles against the British.
item.dc:creator: James Barron
item.category: Museum of the City of New York

item.title: Professor Fired for Criticizing Charlie Kirk Wins $1.9 Million Settlement
item.description: Tamar Shirinian, a former professor at the University of Tennessee at Knoxville, was among those who lost their jobs over their posts about the slain conservative activist.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:credit: Brianna Paciorka/The Knoxville News-Sentinel, via Imagn Images
media:content.media:description: Tamar Shirinian has reached a $1.9 million settlement with the University of Tennessee at Knoxville.

item.title: Minanbé, unas ruinas mayas descubiertas lejos, muy lejos, de los circuitos habituales
item.description: Escaneos aéreos realizados con lidar indicaron la presencia de monumentos y otros vestigios arqueológicos en una zona remota de la península de Yucatán. La travesía para llegar ahí fue ardua.
item.dc:creator: Alan Yuhas and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

item.title: Omán habría propuesto un pago ‘voluntario’ para el estrecho de Ormuz
item.description: A pesar de las objeciones de EE. UU., Irán y Omán avanzan con sus planes de cobrar a los barcos que transiten por el estrecho, según funcionarios y diplomáticos al tanto del asunto.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim, Farnaz Fassihi and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Elke Scholiers/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Un buque de carga anclado frente a la costa de Omán, cerca de Mascate, la semana pasada.

item.title: How to World Cup
item.description: New to soccer? You’re welcome here.
item.dc:creator: Roger Bennett
item.category: United States Men's National Soccer Team

item.title: Democratic Outsiders Keep Rolling: 5 Takeaways From Colorado’s Primaries
item.description: A democratic socialist ousted a veteran congresswoman in Denver, and a U.S. senator lost his bid for governor. But the state’s other senator fended off a progressive primary challenger.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Chet Strange for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Melat Kiros, center, toppled Representative Diana DeGette, a veteran Democrat in Denver.

item.title: Melat Kiros, a Left-Wing Insurgent, Ousts a 15-Term Congresswoman in Colorado
item.description: Melat Kiros, a 29-year-old democratic socialist, unseated Representative Diana DeGette in a Democratic primary to represent the Denver area.
item.dc:creator: Emily Davies

media:content.media:credit: Chet Strange for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Melat Kiros, an immigrant and first-time candidate, is likely to head to Congress in a deep-blue Colorado district.

item.title: Archaeologists Find Maya Monuments Off the Beaten Path. Way Off.
item.description: Scientists drove ATVs for miles and then hiked miles more through dense forest to reach a site untouched by looting on Mexico’s Yucatán Peninsula. They named it Minanbé, meaning “there is no path.”
item.dc:creator: Alan Yuhas and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

item.title: Quote of the Day: G.O.P. Given a Boost Ahead of the Midterms
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, July 1, 2026.

item.title: ¿Cuál es la cuna del fútbol mexicano? Depende a quién le preguntes
item.description: Una pequeña localidad minera se convirtió en destino turístico por su abolengo futbolero. Una ciudad vecina reivindica el mismo legado.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Turistas con camisetas de la selección mexicana en el centro de Mineral del Monte, México, el miércoles.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 1, 2026
item.description: Jeffrey Martinovic bends the laws of crossword physics.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: iStock
media:content.media:description: If solving the Crossword is the extent of your productivity today, you’ll have done plenty.

item.title: Phil Weiser Upsets Michael Bennet in Colorado Democratic Governor Primary
item.description: Phil Weiser beat Senator Michael Bennet in the Democratic primary, presenting himself as the stronger foil for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy

media:content.media:credit: Cheney Orr for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Attorney General Phil Weiser of Colorado speaking with voters during a campaign event at Bluebird Café in Glenwood Springs, Colo.

item.title: Judges Strike Down Trump Rule on Loan Forgiveness for Public Servants
item.description: Two federal courts have blocked a new Trump administration rule that could have narrowed eligibility for a student loan forgiveness program for public servants.
item.dc:creator: Tara Siegel Bernard

media:content.media:credit: Erin Schaff/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Many public servants have staked their financial futures on the loan forgiveness program. Being disqualified could have potentially upended their finances.

item.title: Manny Rutinel, a Progressive, Wins Colorado Primary for Competitive House Seat
item.description: Manny Rutinel, a state representative and former activist, will face Representative Gabe Evans, a Republican seen as vulnerable, in the fall.
item.dc:creator: Emily Davies

media:content.media:credit: Brice Tucker/The Greeley Tribune, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: State Representative Manny Rutinel, a progressive, won his primary in Colorado’s most competitive House district. He will face Representative Gabe Evans, a Republican, in the fall.

item.title: E. Jean Carroll Asks Judge to Order Trump to Pay $5 Million Jury Award
item.description: The writer’s request came after the Supreme Court declined to review a civil case in which President Trump was found liable for sexually abusing and defaming her.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser

media:content.media:credit: Sarah Blesener for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The writer E. Jean Carroll accused President Trump of sexually assaulting her in a Manhattan department store in the 1990s. He denies the accusation and has labeled it a “complete con job.”

item.title: Driver Is Reported Dead Days After Plowing Into World Cup Fans in Mexico
item.description: The episode appears to the most significant violence after wins by the Mexico national team, which has drawn huge crowds onto streets across the country.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman

media:content.media:credit: Ayuntamiento De Los Cabos/Facebook, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Security personnel and emergency responders at the site where a car rammed through a crowd of people in Cabo San Lucas, Mexico, last week.

item.title: U.S. Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Most Powerful A.I. Models
item.description: The move allows Anthropic to bring its most powerful technologies back online, de-escalating a feud with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel and Ana Swanson

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Anthropic’s Code With Claude event in San Francisco last month. Federal restrictions were lifted on Tuesday from the company’s Claude Mythos and Claude Fable A.I. models.

item.title: Trump’s Moneymaking Run: Unrivaled in Presidential History
item.description: The president’s move to open new business ventures, rather than eliminate potential conflicts, defies a long-held tradition.
item.dc:creator: Eric Lipton

media:content.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The bulk of the new revenue for President Trump comes from the cryptocurrency industry, which his family aggressively moved into starting in late 2024.

item.title: Colorado Sixth Congressional District Primary 2026: Live Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Colorado primary election.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Colorado primary election.

item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Colorado primary election.

item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Colorado primary election.

item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Colorado primary election.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Colorado primary election.

item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Colorado primary election.

item.title: Colorado Second Congressional District Primary 2026: Live Election Results
item.description: Get live results and maps from the 2026 Colorado primary election.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: The Dress Code for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding? Expect a Black-Tie Event.
item.description: New details have emerged about the celebration at Madison Square Garden, widely believed to be a wedding, including a dress code.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Chelsia Rose Marcius

media:content.media:credit: Gareth Cattermole/TAS24, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Travis Kelce’s white-tie ensemble here would be a touch too formal for a black-tie event.

item.title: EE. UU. obstaculiza el regreso de María Corina Machado a Venezuela
item.description: La líder opositora exiliada se empeña en volver a un país desolado por los terremotos, lo que ha irritado a las autoridades estadounidenses, enfocadas en la recuperación y la estabilidad.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un grupo de voluntarios civiles busca supervivientes del terremoto en Caribe, Venezuela, el sábado.

item.title: During Traffic Stop, N.F.L. Reporter Appeared to Show Off Her Access
item.description: A body-worn camera recording shows Dianna Russini, formerly an “insider” for The Athletic, talking football with an officer and avoiding a citation.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Rosman and Ken Belson

media:content.media:credit: Geoff Burke/USA TODAY Sports, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Ms. Russini in 2020.

item.description: Body-worn footage appears to show Dianna Russini, a former N.F.L. reporter for The Athletic, showing off her access during a traffic stop in Ridgewood, N.J.

item.title: U.S. Mobilizes for Venezuela Despite Trump’s Disdain for Foreign Aid
item.description: The State Department has promised $100 million in new funds to aid groups, after President Trump was criticized for an anemic response to an earthquake in Myanmar last year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Edward Wong
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
United States Agency for International Development

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Volunteers and family members searched for survivors amid several collapsed residential buildings in La Guaira, Venezuela, on Monday. Two powerful earthquakes struck the region last week.

item.title: 5 Temperature Records Meteorologists Are Watching This Week
item.description: New York could record 100 degrees for the first time since 2012, as a brutal heat wave spreads east.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Washington might break records for heat this week.

item.title: 10-Year-Old Boy Charged With First-Degree Murder in Shooting of Infant Niece
item.description: The boy shot his 7-month-old niece at a St. Louis home with a firearm that had been kept under a mattress, the police said. The baby’s father, who owned the weapon, has also been charged.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:credit: James A. Finley/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The Mel Carnahan Courthouse in St. Louis, one of the two buildings where the 22nd Judicial Circuit Court of Missouri has offices.

item.title: In Copenhagen, a Pioneering Shop Owner Marks Her Next Chapter
item.description: The owner of the Apartment, which introduced the concept of residential retail, invited friends for a dinner in her new gallery and guesthouse.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Farrell

item.title: In the United States, Every World Cup Team Is a Home Team
item.description: At block parties, backyards and beer halls, an expanded tournament gave immigrant diasporas large and small the chance to revel in support of their home countries.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times

item.title: Colorado Handyman Discovers Bear in His Truck Eating His Lunch
item.description: A handyman in Steamboat Springs, Colo., discovered a bear eating his lunch in his truck.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: For Many Americans, Depression Is Familiar. Here’s What We Know.
item.description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr. announced he had been hospitalized for depression. More than one in four U.S. adults report having been diagnosed with the condition.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein and Dana G. Smith

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Thomas Kean Jr. appeared on Capitol Hill on Tuesday following a lengthy absence.

item.title: Butter Rice Cakes
item.description: Also known as Shanghai butter mochi, Shanghai butter rice cakes and butter tteok in Korea, these butter mochi are individually sized, extra-crunchy and have taken the internet by storm.

media:content.media:credit: Linda Xiao for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.

item.title: Nicolás Maduro es demandado en EE. UU. por asesinatos de 5 jóvenes en Caracas
item.description: Este caso representa un intento poco habitual de hacer responsable a un jefe de Estado ante un tribunal de Estados Unidos.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una demandante cubierta para ocultar su identidad. La demanda se presentó utilizando seudónimos para proteger a las familias.

item.title: After a Tap Heard in Rubble, U.S. Rescue Teams Begin a Grueling Marathon
item.description: Surrounded by the devastation of Venezuela’s earthquakes, emergency specialists from California, Virginia and Florida work with locals to search for survivors.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler and Billy Witz

media:content.media:credit: Matias Delacroix/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Members of the Fairfax County Urban Search and Rescue team from Virginia searched for people in the rubble of a collapsed building in La Guaira, Venezuela, on Sunday.

item.title: Trump Pulled in at Least $2.2 Billion in 2025, Financial Disclosure Shows
item.description: The release of a mandatory financial disclosure for 2025 shows that the Trump family’s holdings, particularly the president’s crypto businesses, were stunningly lucrative.
item.dc:creator: Ben Protess, Andrea Fuller, Eric Lipton and David Yaffe-Bellany

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump entering the White House on Sunday. His mandatory financial disclosure report for 2025 pulled back the curtain on his secretive business operations.
